title: polygonum neglectum subsp. rectum
intro: Polygonum neglectum subsp. rectum, also known as bistort or knotweed, is a perennial plant species in the Polygonaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms associated with diarrhea, wounds, and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Polygonum neglectum subsp. rectum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
